## Workflows update Step

`<slack:get-workflowsupdate-step>`

Update the configuration for a workflow extension step. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /workflows.updateStep endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Step name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional field used to override the step name that is shown in the Workflow Builder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>outputs</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>JSON array of output objects used during step execution. This is the data your app agrees to provide when your workflow step is executed.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Workflow step edit ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Context identifier provided with <code>view_submission</code> payloads used to call back to <code>workflows.updateStep</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>inputs</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>JSON key-value map of inputs required from a user during configuration. This is the data your app expects receive when the workflow step starts. The embedded variable format is set and replaced by the workflow system. You canât create custom variables that are replaced at runtime. For more information about variables in workflow steps, refer to the <a href="https://docs.slack.dev/changelog/2023-08-workflow-steps-from-apps-step-back/">Slack API documentation</a>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Step image URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Optional field used to override the app image that is shown in the Workflow Builder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit that qualifies the Response Timeout.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Defines whether to send the request using streaming. If the value is set to <code>AUTO</code> the best strategy is automatically determined based on the content of the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable in which to store the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- SLACK:BAD_REQUEST
- SLACK:CLIENT_ERROR
- SLACK:CONNECTIVITY
- SLACK:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- SLACK:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- SLACK:NOT_FOUND
- SLACK:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SLACK:SERVER_ERROR
- SLACK:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- SLACK:TIMEOUT
- SLACK:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- SLACK:UNAUTHORIZED
- SLACK:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Unauthorize

`<slack:unauthorize>`

Deletes all the access token information of a given resource owner id so that itâs impossible to
execute any operation for that user without doing the authorization dance again

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The id of the resource owner which access should be invalidated</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

## On New File Trigger

`<slack:on-new-file-trigger>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created timestamp</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timestamp since when to query new files</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the channel</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The ID of the user</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in Cluster</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scheduling Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

## On New Message Trigger

`<slack:on-new-message-trigger>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created timestamp</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timestamp value as lower bound for new messages</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Conversation ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the channel, DM or group DM to poll</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Limit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximal number of records to retrieve</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in Cluster</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scheduling Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configuration](#Config)

## Types

### Default Header

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Default Query Param

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Proxy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Non Proxy Hosts</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Tls

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Enabled Protocols</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enabled Cipher Suites</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trust Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Revocation Check</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom Ocsp Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">Crl File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the trust store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Insecure</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If true, no certificate validations will be performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The type of store used.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the key store contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file will be used by default.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to protect the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The algorithm used by the key store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Only End Entities</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prefer Crls</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>No Fallback</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Soft Fail</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom Ocsp Responder

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The URL of the OCSP responder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cert Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response (must be in the trust store), if present.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Crl File

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The path to the CRL file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fails Deployment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment will fail if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The reconnection strategy to use</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often (in ms) to reconnect</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Blocking</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How many reconnection attempts to make</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often (in ms) to reconnect</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Blocking</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, the reconnection strategy will run in a separate, non-blocking thread</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Idle Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>A scalar time value for the maximum amount of time a dynamic configuration instance should be allowed to be idle before itâs considered eligible for expiration</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A time unit that qualifies the maxIdleTime attribute</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Http Response Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Status Code</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reason Phrase</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the amount of memory that will be allocated in order to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can be fit into this buffer, then it will be expanded by according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is by how much will be buffer size by expanded if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower will mean that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised when the buffer gets full.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the maximum amount of memory that will be used. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which all these attributes are expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Memory Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines the maximum memory that the stream should use to keep data in memory. If more than that is consumed then it will start to buffer the content on disk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Redelivery Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of times a message can be redelivered and processed unsuccessfully before triggering process-failed-message</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Digest Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The secure hashing algorithm to use. If not set, the default is SHA-256.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Identifier</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines which strategy is used to identify the messages.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><p>The object store where the redelivery counter for each message is going to be stored.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Use Secure Hash</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to use a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Id Expression</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property may only be set if useSecureHash is false.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the amount of instances that will be initially be allowed to be kept in memory in order to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then it will be expanded according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize. Default value is 100 instances.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is by how much will the buffer size by expanded if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower will mean that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the maximum amount of memory that will be used. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error will be raised. A value lower or equal to zero means no limit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Memory Objects</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is the maximum amount of instances that will be kept in memory. If more than that is required, then it will start to buffer the content on disk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The unit in which maxInMemorySize is expressed</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Slack Connector 2.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems in real time, as
described in [View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Slack Connector Reference](slack-connector-reference).

For an end-to-end demo about configuring Slack Connector, watch the
[Setting Up Slack Connector in Anypoint Studio](https://videos.mulesoft.com/watch/oePSFB16o26jq7GUyWAAMt)
Friends of Max video.

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Anypoint Connector for Slack (Slack Connector) to your Mule project to automatically populate
the XML code with the connectorâs namespace and schema location and to add the required dependencies
to the projectâs `pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In the **Add Dependencies to Project** window, type `slack` in the search field.
3.  Click **Slack Connector** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. Configure one of these sources to use
with Slack Connector:

- HTTP Listener

  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port

- Scheduler

  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP Listener source, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP > Listener**.
2.  Drag **Listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  Specify a value for the **Path** field.
5.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the HTTP Listener source in the app.
6.  In the **General** tab, specify the connection information for the connector.
7.  In the **Security** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
8.  In the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
10. Click **OK**.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you are specifying an action for that connector to
perform.

To add an operation for Slack Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Slack** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas and to the right of the source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the
authentication credentials that the connector requires to access the target Slack system. Slack
Connector supports OAuth 2.0 authentication, which delegates user authentication to the service
hosting the user account.

To configure the global element for Slack Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the name of the connector in the Studio canvas.
2.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
3.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to access the global element configuration fields.
4.  On the **General** tab, complete the following fields to set up OAuth 2.0 authentication:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer key</strong></p></td><td><p>OAuth consumer key as registered with the service provider.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer secret</strong></p></td><td><p>OAuth consumer secret as registered with the service provider.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorization url</strong></p></td><td><p>Service providerâs authorization endpoint URL.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access token url</strong></p></td><td><p>Service providerâs access token endpoint URL.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scopes</strong></p></td><td><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. If not provided, this value defaults to those in the annotation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Resource owner Id</strong></p></td><td><p>Resource owner ID that each component should use if it doesnât otherwise reference one.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Before</strong></p></td><td><p>Name of a flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>After</strong></p></td><td><p>Name of a flow to execute immediately after an access token is received.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Listener config</strong></p></td><td><p>Reference to an HTTP listener configuration to use to create the listener that receives the access token callback endpoint.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Callback path</strong></p></td><td><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorize path</strong></p></td><td><p>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>External callback url</strong></p></td><td><p>If the callback endpoint is behind a proxy or must be accessed through a non-direct URL, use this parameter to tell the OAuth provider the URL to use to access the callback.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object store</strong></p></td><td><p>Reference to the object store to use to store each resource owner IDâs data. If not specified, Mule automatically provisions the default object store.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
    (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
    properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
    configure them, see
    [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

5.  On the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
6.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
7.  Click **OK**.

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for Slack Connector, configure the other required fields for
the connector. The required fields vary depending on which connector operation you use.

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
  console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log is visible in your OS
  console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

## Next Step

After configuring an app in Studio, see the [Slack Examples](slack-connector-examples) section to
experiment with the Slack connector.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Slack Connector Reference](slack-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Upgrading and Migrating Slack Connector to Version 2.x

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Upgrade Anypoint Connector for Slack (Slack Connector) to version 2.x.

## Supported Upgrade Paths

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>From Version</th><th>To Version</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>1.0</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>2.x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Changes in This Release

This release contains the following changes:

- This connector no longer has the **Upload File** operation because the Slack `files.upload` API
  method is deprecated. For more information, refer to the
  [Slack API documentation](https://docs.slack.dev/reference/methods/files.upload/).
- To upload files, this connector now includes the new **Get upload url** and **Complete Upload
  External** operations.

  For more information about the operations, refer to
  [Get Upload URL](slack-connector-reference#GetFilesUploadUrl) and
  [Complete Upload External](slack-connector-reference#CreateFilesCompleteuploadExternal).

  For examples about how to use these operations, refer to
  [Upload a File to a Slack Channel (Using File Path)](slack-connector-examples#upload-file-to-channel)
  and
  [Upload File Text Content to a Slack Channel](slack-connector-examples#upload-file-text-content-to-channel).

- In the Slack Connector configuration window, the **TLS** tab is renamed to the **Security** tab.

## Upgrade Prerequisites

Before you perform the upgrade, create a backup of your files, data, and configurations in case you
need to restore to them.

## Upgrade Steps

Follow these steps to perform the upgrade:

1.  In Anypoint Studio, create a Mule project.
2.  In **Mule Palette**, click **Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, enter `slack` in the search field.
4.  In **Available modules**, select **Slack** and click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.
6.  Verify that the Slack Connector dependency version is 2.x in the `pom.xml` file in the Mule
    project.

Studio upgrades the connector automatically.

### Verify the Upgrade

After you install the latest version of the connector, verify the upgrade:

1.  In Studio, verify that there are no errors in the **Problems** or **Console** views.
2.  Verify that there are no problems in the project `pom.xml` file.
3.  Test the connection to verify that the operations work.

### Reverting the Upgrade

If it is necessary to revert to the previous version of Slack Connector, change the mule-slack
dependency version in the projectâs `pom.xml` file to the previous version.

You must update the projectâs `pom.xml` file in Anypoint Studio.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Slack 2.0 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio:

- If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML, either from the Anypoint Studio XML
  editor or from a text editor, you can access the connector from your app by adding reference
  statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.
- If you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
  connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/slack
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/slack/current/mule-slack.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the `<mule>` XML block:

```xml
<mule xmlns:<connector>="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/slack"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/slack
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/slack/current/mule-slack.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
If you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to
this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>GROUP_ID_FROM_EXCHANGE</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-slack-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `slack`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## Next Step

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the
[Slack Connector Examples](slack-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
